content:
{
  "title": "Contextual Cross-Language Prompting for Multilingual LLMs via Synthetic Pivot Translations",
  "problem": "Large language models often underperform on low-resource languages due to limited training data and prompt misalignment. Existing multilingual prompting methods typically rely on direct translation or language-specific prompts, which fail to leverage shared linguistic features across languages.",
  "motivation": "Improving LLM performance on low-resource languages addresses critical equity gaps and expands the accessibility of AI. Recent advances in zero-shot and translation-based prompting have limitations when linguistic coverage is sparse. Now, more cross-lingual resources and synthetic data generation tools enable novel prompt engineering strategies.",
  "method": "We propose a prompting method that augments low-resource language prompts with synthetic 'pivot' translations in linguistically related high-resource languages. For each input, we generate prompts containing the original text, its translation in a phylogenetically proximate language (the 'pivot'), and guiding context highlighting shared grammatical or lexical cues. The prompt instructs the LLM to jointly consider both versions, leveraging transfer from high-resource pivots to improve reasoning and generation in the target language. This method is model-agnostic and does not require retraining.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on FLORES and XTREME multilingual benchmarks, focusing on low-resource language subsets. Baselines will include direct prompting, translation-based prompting, and multilingual chain-of-thought methods. Metrics will be accuracy, BLEU/F1 (for generation), and cross-lingual consistency. Ablations will compare different pivot selection strategies and prompt formats."
}
